footer.description: HateCheck is an AI-powered intelligence platform that detects and classifies antisemitism and hate speech across the Netherlands and Europe.

hero.title: Detect. Classify. Act.
hero.subtitle: HateCheck is Europe's leading intelligence platform for detecting, classifying, and responding to antisemitism and online hate speech — built on IHRA standards, Dutch law, and cutting-edge AI.

problem.label: The Problem
problem.col1Desc: Thousands of incidents occur daily across social media, news sites, and messaging platforms — far beyond manual review capacity. The sheer volume makes manual monitoring impossible.
problem.col2Desc: Much hate speech hides behind satire, coded language, and plausible deniability. Classification is difficult, inconsistent, and varies across legal jurisdictions.
problem.col3Desc: Communities lack accessible tools to document, classify, and report incidents in ways that are actionable for authorities and organizations.

howItWorks.label: Methodology
howItWorks.step1Desc: URLs, text, images, or live feeds submitted for analysis across 20+ platforms and formats.
howItWorks.step2Desc: Content is verified for authenticity and relevance before entering the analysis pipeline.
howItWorks.step3Desc: Multi-model AI applies semantic, contextual, and visual analysis against established frameworks.
howItWorks.step4Desc: Five-tier severity assignment based on IHRA, JDA, Nexus, and Dutch legal standards.
howItWorks.step5Desc: Generates referral packages, reports, and documentation for authorities and organizations.

tiers.label: Classification System
tiers.tier1Desc: Content that does not meet the threshold for antisemitism under any applicable standard.
tiers.tier1Response: No action required. Archive for reference.
tiers.tier2Desc: Borderline content that may be antisemitic depending on context, intent, and audience.
tiers.tier2Response: Flag for human review and contextual analysis.
tiers.tier3Desc: Content that qualifies as antisemitic under IHRA/JDA definitions but does not cross criminal thresholds.
tiers.tier3Response: Document, report to platform, notify monitoring bodies.
tiers.tier4Desc: Content potentially violating Dutch Penal Code Articles 137c, 137d, or 137e.
tiers.tier4Response: Formal referral to law enforcement with evidence package.
tiers.tier5Desc: Immediate threat indicators requiring urgent referral to law enforcement.
tiers.tier5Response: Emergency referral. Notify targets and authorities immediately.

credibility.label: Standards & Frameworks
credibility.description: Grounded in established international frameworks and Dutch legal standards.

quote.text: If there is a bedrock principle underlying the First Amendment, it is that government may not prohibit the expression of an idea simply because society finds the idea offensive or disagreeable.
quote.attribution: Justice Louis D. Brandeis — dissent in Whitney v. California (1927)

product.title: The HateCheck Platform
product.subtitle: A comprehensive intelligence system for detecting, classifying, and acting on antisemitism and hate speech.
product.step1Desc: Submit URLs, text, images, or connect data streams. Supports 20+ platforms and formats.
product.step2Desc: Multi-model AI analyzes content for antisemitic tropes, coded language, and explicit hatred.
product.step3Desc: Automatic mapping to IHRA, JDA, Nexus, and Dutch Penal Code (137c, 137d, 137e).
product.step4Desc: Assigns one of 5 tiers based on severity, context, and legal framework applicability.
product.step5Desc: Generates referral packages, reports, documentation for authorities and organizations.

standards.title: Legal & Definitional Standards
standards.subtitle: HateCheck's classification methodology is grounded in internationally recognized definitions and Dutch law.

about.title: About HateCheck
about.subtitle: We built HateCheck because the problem demanded a serious response.

organizations.title: For Organizations
organizations.subtitle: HateCheck empowers institutions to detect, classify, and respond to hate speech at scale.

resources.title: Resources & Research
resources.subtitle: Analysis, guides, and research on antisemitism, hate speech, and digital intelligence.

reports.title: Incident Reports
reports.subtitle: Anonymized case studies demonstrating HateCheck classification in action.

contact.subtitle: Contact us for demos, partnerships, research inquiries, or general questions.
